1.1.section: 1. Data description and collection or re-use of existing data
1.1.question: How will new data be collected or produced and/or how will existing data be re-used?
1.1.content:
Dół formularza
Electrochemical data will be acquired using various electrochemical techniques, including Dynamic Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (DEIS), Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (EIS), Square Wave Voltammetry (SWV), Cyclic Voltammetry (CV) and Chronoamperometry (CA), in accordance with methodologies described in relevant scientific publications and according to the recommendations of the equipment manufacturers. Raw data will be recorded using dedicated electrochemical software and subsequently exported into open formats such as CSV and TXT files for further analysis and long term preservation. Processed datasets generated during multivariate data analysis, including Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) and Principal Component Analysis (PCA), will also constitute part of the research data. Data files will be segregated according to the performed experiments and organized into separate folders corresponding to the measurement conditions and acquisition dates.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Dół formularza

Electrochemical data will be acquired using various electrochemical techniques, including Dynamic Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (DEIS), Electrochemical Impedance Spectroscopy (EIS), Square Wave Voltammetry (SWV), Cyclic Voltammetry (CV) and Chronoamperometry (CA), in accordance with methodologies described in relevant scientific publications and according to the recommendations of the equipment manufacturers. Raw data will be recorded using dedicated electrochemical software and subsequently exported into open formats such as CSV and TXT files for further analysis and long term preservation. Processed datasets generated during multivariate data analysis, including Singular Value Decomposition (SVD) and Principal Component Analysis (PCA), will also constitute part of the research data. Data files will be segregated according to the performed experiments and organized into separate folders corresponding to the measurement conditions and acquisition dates.

1.2.section: 1. Data description and collection or re-use of existing data
1.2.question: What data (for example the types, formats, and volumes) will be collected or produced?
1.2.content: The project will generate electrochemical and physicochemical datasets, including raw impedance spectra, current and potential responses, equivalent circuit fitting parameters, multivariate statistical analysis outputs, and graphical data representations. The data will be stored in open and commonly used formats, including CSV, TXT, XLSX, PDF, PNG, and SVG files. Raw data acquired using dedicated electrochemical instrumentation software will be exported into interoperable formats for further analysis and long term preservation. Processed datasets generated during multivariate data analysis, including Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and Singular Value Decomposition (SVD), will also constitute part of the research data. Due to the large size of time resolved DEIS measurements, the total volume of generated data is expected not to exceed approximately 1 TB, depending on the number and duration of experiments.

tagged_paragraphs.text: The project will generate electrochemical and physicochemical datasets, including raw impedance spectra, current and potential responses, equivalent circuit fitting parameters, multivariate statistical analysis outputs, and graphical data representations. The data will be stored in open and commonly used formats, including CSV, TXT, XLSX, PDF, PNG, and SVG files. Raw data acquired using dedicated electrochemical instrumentation software will be exported into interoperable formats for further analysis and long term preservation. Processed datasets generated during multivariate data analysis, including Principal Component Analysis (PCA) and Singular Value Decomposition (SVD), will also constitute part of the research data. Due to the large size of time resolved DEIS measurements, the total volume of generated data is expected not to exceed approximately 1 TB, depending on the number and duration of experiments.

2.1.question: What metadata and documentation (for example methodology or data collection and way of organising data) will accompany data?
2.1.content:
Dół formularza
Data files will be organized according to the performed experiments and segregated into folders corresponding to the measurement technique, stressor type, analyte concentration, and acquisition date. File names will include information regarding experimental conditions and measurement dates. Metadata describing instrumental parameters, experimental conditions, preprocessing procedures, and data analysis workflows will be maintained in README files accompanying the datasets. Experimental methodologies and data processing procedures will be documented to ensure reproducibility and traceability between raw and processed datasets.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Dół formularza

Data files will be organized according to the performed experiments and segregated into folders corresponding to the measurement technique, stressor type, analyte concentration, and acquisition date. File names will include information regarding experimental conditions and measurement dates. Metadata describing instrumental parameters, experimental conditions, preprocessing procedures, and data analysis workflows will be maintained in README files accompanying the datasets. Experimental methodologies and data processing procedures will be documented to ensure reproducibility and traceability between raw and processed datasets.

2.2.question: What data quality control measures will be used?
2.2.content: Data quality will be ensured through instrument calibration, repeated measurements, and the use of standardized experimental protocols based on peer reviewed literature and manufacturer recommendations. Quality control procedures will include validation of equivalent circuit fitting, monitoring of fitting residuals and fitting errors, comparison of replicate measurements, and consistency checks between different electrochemical techniques. Data preprocessing and multivariate analysis workflows will be verified before final interpretation of the results.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Data quality will be ensured through instrument calibration, repeated measurements, and the use of standardized experimental protocols based on peer reviewed literature and manufacturer recommendations. Quality control procedures will include validation of equivalent circuit fitting, monitoring of fitting residuals and fitting errors, comparison of replicate measurements, and consistency checks between different electrochemical techniques. Data preprocessing and multivariate analysis workflows will be verified before final interpretation of the results.

3.1.section: 3. Storage and backup during the research process
3.1.question: How will data and metadata be stored and backed up during the research process?
3.1.content:
Dół formularza
Data and metadata will be stored on secured institutional computers, external storage devices, and in the Gdańsk Tech OneDrive cloud environment accessible only to authorized project participants. The 3 2 1 backup rule will be applied, including storage on local devices and cloud infrastructure. Backups will be performed regularly throughout the project duration. The project does not plan to collect sensitive personal data. If such data are acquired unexpectedly, they will be encrypted and stored in accordance with the Gdańsk Tech data security policy and GDPR regulations.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Dół formularza

The 3 2 1 backup rule will be applied, including storage on local devices and cloud infrastructure. Backups will be performed regularly throughout the project duration. The project does not plan to collect sensitive personal data. If such data are acquired unexpectedly, they will be encrypted and stored in accordance with the Gdańsk Tech data security policy and GDPR regulations.

3.2.section: 3. Storage and backup during the research process
3.2.question: How will data security and protection of sensitive data be taken care of during the research?
3.2.content: Research data will be stored on password protected institutional computers and in the secure Gdańsk Tech OneDrive cloud environment accessible only to authorized project participants. Regular backups will be performed throughout the project. The project does not plan to collect personal or sensitive data. If such data are acquired unexpectedly, they will be encrypted, access restricted, and processed in accordance with the applicable data protection regulations and institutional policies.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Research data will be stored on password protected institutional computers and in the secure Gdańsk Tech OneDrive cloud environment accessible only to authorized project participants. Regular backups will be performed throughout the project. The project does not plan to collect personal or sensitive data. If such data are acquired unexpectedly, they will be encrypted, access restricted, and processed in accordance with the applicable data protection regulations and institutional policies.

4.1.question: If personal data are processed, how will compliance with legislation on personal data and on data security be ensured?
4.1.content:
Dół formularza
The project does not involve the planned collection or processing of personal data. Should personal data be acquired unexpectedly, all processing activities will be conducted in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the internal data protection policies of Gdańsk University of Technology. Access to such data will be limited to authorized personnel only.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Dół formularza

The project does not involve the planned collection or processing of personal data. Should personal data be acquired unexpectedly, all processing activities will be conducted in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the internal data protection policies of Gdańsk University of Technology. Access to such data will be limited to authorized personnel only.

4.2.question: How will other legal issues, such as intellectual property rights and ownership, be managed? What legislation is applicable?
4.2.content: Ownership of research data and intellectual property rights arising from the project will be managed in accordance with the Regulations concerning management of copyright, related rights, industrial property rights, and commercialization rules at Gdańsk University of Technology. Data sharing procedures will comply with the NCN Open Access Policy and FAIR data management principles. Research datasets deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalogue will be made available under the CC BY 4.0 license unless other licensing conditions are required. Any restrictions related to intellectual property protection will be clearly specified prior to data publication.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Ownership of research data and intellectual property rights arising from the project will be managed in accordance with the Regulations concerning management of copyright, related rights, industrial property rights, and commercialization rules at Gdańsk University of Technology. Data sharing procedures will comply with the NCN Open Access Policy and FAIR data management principles. Research datasets deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalogue will be made available under the CC BY 4.0 license unless other licensing conditions are required. Any restrictions related to intellectual property protection will be clearly specified prior to data publication.

5.1.question: How and when will data be shared? Are there possible restrictions to data sharing or embargo reasons?
5.1.content:
Dół formularza
Selected research data will be deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalogue and made publicly available no later than upon publication of the related research article. Access to unpublished datasets may be temporarily restricted to protect publication priority and intellectual property rights. Any embargo period will be limited to the minimum time necessary to ensure proper dissemination of research results.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Dół formularza

Selected research data will be deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalogue and made publicly available no later than upon publication of the related research article. Access to unpublished datasets may be temporarily restricted to protect publication priority and intellectual property rights. Any embargo period will be limited to the minimum time necessary to ensure proper dissemination of research results.

5.2.question: How will data for preservation be selected, and where will data be preserved long-term (for example a data repository or archive)?
5.2.content: Data selected for long term preservation will include raw electrochemical measurements, processed datasets, metadata, data analysis workflows, and supporting documentation necessary for interpretation and reuse of the results. Long term preservation will be ensured through deposition in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalogue and institutional storage infrastructure provided by Gdańsk University of Technology. Data not deposited in repositories will be archived for at least 10 years.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Data selected for long term preservation will include raw electrochemical measurements, processed datasets, metadata, data analysis workflows, and supporting documentation necessary for interpretation and reuse of the results. Long term preservation will be ensured through deposition in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalogue and institutional storage infrastructure provided by Gdańsk University of Technology. Data not deposited in repositories will be archived for at least 10 years.

5.3.question: What methods or software tools will be needed to access and use the data?
5.3.content: Data will be stored in widely used and interoperable formats, including CSV, TXT, XLSX, PDF, PNG, and SVG. Access to the datasets will require only standard spreadsheet, text editing, and document viewing software. Data interpretation may require domain specific knowledge in electrochemistry and electrochemical impedance analysis. Where applicable, descriptions of data processing workflows will be provided in accompanying documentation.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Data will be stored in widely used and interoperable formats, including CSV, TXT, XLSX, PDF, PNG, and SVG. Access to the datasets will require only standard spreadsheet, text editing, and document viewing software. Data interpretation may require domain specific knowledge in electrochemistry and electrochemical impedance analysis. Where applicable, descriptions of data processing workflows will be provided in accompanying documentation.

5.4.question: How will the application of a unique and persistent identifier (such as a Digital Object Identifier (DOI)) to each data set be ensured?
5.4.content: Datasets deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalogue will receive a persistent Digital Object Identifier (DOI), ensuring long term discoverability, accessibility, and citability. Metadata accompanying the datasets will facilitate identification and reuse of the deposited research outputs.

tagged_paragraphs.text: Datasets deposited in the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalogue will receive a persistent Digital Object Identifier (DOI), ensuring long term discoverability, accessibility, and citability. Metadata accompanying the datasets will facilitate identification and reuse of the deposited research outputs.

6.1.question: Who (for example role, position, and institution) will be responsible for data management (i.e. the data steward)?
6.1.content:
Dół formularza
The Principal Investigator will be responsible for data management, including data organization, documentation, storage, and sharing procedures. Project participants will contribute to maintaining data quality and metadata completeness. Institutional infrastructure provided by Gdańsk University of Technology, including OneDrive cloud storage and the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalogue, will be used for long term data preservation and sharing. Data management activities will be supported using project resources and indirect costs when necessary, including storage space and software required for data processing and analysis

tagged_paragraphs.text: Dół formularza

The Principal Investigator will be responsible for data management, including data organization, documentation, storage, and sharing procedures. Project participants will contribute to maintaining data quality and metadata completeness. Institutional infrastructure provided by Gdańsk University of Technology, including OneDrive cloud storage and the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalogue, will be used for long term data preservation and sharing. Data management activities will be supported using project resources and indirect costs when necessary, including storage space and software required for data processing and analysis

6.2.question: What resources (for example financial and time) will be dedicated to data management and ensuring the data will be FAIR (Findable, Accessible, Interoperable, Re-usable)?
6.2.content: Data management activities will be supported through institutional infrastructure provided by Gdańsk University of Technology, including OneDrive cloud storage and the MOST Wiedzy Open Research Data Catalogue. Time required for data organization, documentation, backup creation, metadata preparation, and repository deposition will be included in the regular activities of project participants. Any additional costs associated with data storage, processing, or sharing will be covered from project resources, including indirect costs where applicable.
